mnemonics: HALF: A Hyperplane Always Finds a separation for non-intersecting convex sets.
keyPoints: A convex set is one where the line segment between any two points lies entirely within the set.
A separating hyperplane exists for two disjoint convex sets, creating a plane that divides space so each set lies on opposite sides.
A function is convex if the line segment between any two points on the function lies on or above the graph of the function (the epigraph is a convex set).
commonMistakes: Assuming a hyperplane can always separate any two disjoint sets (they must be convex and disjoint).
Confusing the conditions for a convex function (line segment test) with those for a convex set.
